title: A Simple Procedure to Help Keep Weight Off After Stopping Medicine
summary: Many people take medicines like Ozempic to lose weight. But when they stop taking the medicine, they often gain the weight back. A new study shows that a simple procedure might help people keep the weight off. The procedure is called duodenal mucosal resurfacing. It uses heat to fix the inside of the small intestine. This helps the body stay healthy and not gain back the weight. In the study, people who had the procedure gained back less weight than people who did not have it. The procedure is safe and people can go back to their normal activities in about one day. This could help many people who want to keep their weight off without taking medicine forever.

background_read: Many people take special medicines to help them lose weight. These medicines work well, but when people stop taking them, they often gain the weight back. Doctors are looking for ways to help people keep the weight off without taking medicine forever. One new idea is a simple procedure that uses heat to fix part of the intestine. This might help the body stay healthy and not gain back the weight. The procedure is quick and safe, and people can go back to their normal life in about a day.
Article_Structure: WHO: People who have lost weight using medicines like Ozempic and want to keep it off.
WHAT: A procedure called duodenal mucosal resurfacing that uses heat to fix the intestine and help maintain weight loss.
WHERE: The procedure is done in a hospital or clinic, and it affects the small intestine.
WHY: To help people avoid regaining weight after stopping weight loss medicines.

perspectives.description: Patients want to keep their weight off without taking medicine forever. This procedure offers a possible solution.
Doctors see this as a new way to help patients maintain weight loss and improve health.
